title: Delta Force

gpu: AMD Radeon RX 580 (radeonsi, polaris10, ACO, DRM 3.64, 6.16.7-zen1-1-zen)
notes:
This is because the ACE version of Delta Force was updated and now checks the integrity of the anti-cheat files. The Strinova version of ACE is outdated and isn't compatible with the new one; therefore, until Strinova (or another game with ACE and Linux support) updates its ACE version, the game will remain unplayable.

But it's not fair because the game works on Steam Deck and not on Desktop Linux just because developers are still being childish regarding Linux in the big 2026. They think that adding Linux support would imply more hackers, when history has shown that kernel-level anti-cheats only damage the game's reputation and the possibility for Linux users to play---and we already have a hard time hacking games because it's not as simple as it is on Windows (besides, regarding the latter, kernel-level anti-cheats haven't stopped hackers in any game anyway).

If it works on Steam Deck, make it work on Desktop Linux; using a Deck with a dock is the exact same thing as Desktop Linux.
title: Delta Force

notes: The games makes use of anti-cheat, and I guess that's the main reason it fails.
